sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Establish a one-year independent Cardano dOSPO and Open Maintenance Framework program to manage open source sustainability funds, advisory councils, a public charity target entity, maintenance retainers, maintainer development, bounties, and ecosystem activation programs.
Recipient: Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting as sole Article II.7.5 administrator at treasury withdrawal, with Mill Law Firm designated as independent financial auditor.

Deliverables: Four programs: Maintenance Fund, Maintainer Development program, CodeForUs bounty program, and Ecosystem Activation Reserve, Two advisory councils chartered with members named, COI policies signed, first meeting held, and charters published on-chain by week 6, dOSPO legal entity constituted as a 501(c)(3) public charity with governance documents published and operator agreement executed by week 24, Quarterly outcome reports for all active programs published on-chain with Mill Law Firm financial review attached, Dependency audit, SBOMs, centrality scores, bus factor assessments, and coverage gap analysis for Cardano OSS stack, First retainer cohort selected with published rubric scores, signed agreements, and on-chain disbursements, Public CHAOSS-aligned health dashboard for funded projects, End-of-term portfolio review with financial audit attached
Deadline/expiry: 12 months, with milestones at weeks 6, 12, 13, 16, 18, 24, 26, 39, and 52
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months for a revised Cardano dOSPO and OMF program.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting is designated as the sole Article II.7.5 funds administrator effective at the moment of treasury withdrawal.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mill Law Firm is designated as independent financial auditor for the full term on a quarterly cadence.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The advisory councils will provide published feedback before each disbursement, but final allocation authority remains with the designated administrator.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says a dedicated dOSPO legal entity structured as a U.S. 501(c)(3) public charity will be formed by Month 6, subject to legal processes and governance.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Maintenance Fund will deploy 2,000,000 ADA over 12 months to fund high-risk Cardano open source infrastructure selected through dependency centrality and bus-factor analysis.
missing: Independent evidence for: Mill Law Firm is designated as independent financial auditor for the full term on a quarterly cadence.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says a dedicated dOSPO legal entity structured as a U.S. 501(c)(3) public charity will be formed by Month 6, subject to legal processes and governance.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims the dOSPO and OMF whitepapers were developed with experts from Bitergia, CHAOSS, Apache Software Foundation, Linux Foundation Decentralized Trust, Intersect, Andamio Platform, Modus Create, Leadingbit Solutions, and the Chinstrap Community.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months for a revised Cardano dOSPO and OMF program." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Mill Law Firm is designated as independent financial auditor for the full term on a quarterly cadence." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
